title: EEE-Bench 开放题
rewritten_question_text: The figure shows the scenario of power transmission to a user through a transmission line. The step-up transformer \(T_1\) and the step-down transformer \(T_2\) are both considered ideal transformers, and the resistance of the transmission line is \(R\). Based on this setup, what is the correct statement?
expected_answer: The output power of \( T_1 \) is greater than the input power of \( T_2 \)

title: EEE-Bench 开放题
rewritten_question_text: In the single-phase rectifier consisting of three thyristors \(T_1\), \(T_2\), \(T_3\) and a diode \(D_1\) shown in the figure, the circuit feeds power to a \(10 \, \text{A}\) constant current load. \(T_1\) and \(T_3\) are fired at \(\alpha = 60^\circ\) and \(T_2\) is fired at \(\alpha = 240^\circ\). The reference for \(\alpha\) is the positive zero crossing of \(V_{\text{in}}\). What is the average voltage \(V_o\) across the load in volts? Round off to 2 decimal places.

title: EEE-Bench 子题 1
rewritten_question_text: A Boolean digital circuit is composed using two 4-input multiplexers (M1 and M2) and one 2-input multiplexer (M3), as shown in the figure. X0-X7 are the inputs of the multiplexers M1 and M2 and could be connected to either 0 or 1. The select lines of the multiplexers are connected to Boolean variables A, B, and C as shown in the figure. To realize the Boolean function \(\overline{A} + \overline{A} \cdot \overline{C} + A \cdot \overline{B} \cdot C\), what should be the value of X0?
